phrase: You MUST call the `agr_curation_query` tool before providing any response about genes.
synonyms: call at least one of agr_curation_query before final output
reason: GV-RTC / GV-03 de-dup. The pre-rewrite '## Tool Requirement' restated the required-tool-call imperative that the locked Generated Runtime Contract already injects ('Required tool-call policy: call at least one of agr_curation_query before final output'). The rewrite drops the base imperative and keeps the curator-facing success line 'You called agr_curation_query before providing any gene information' once; the machine imperative survives in the core half of the assembled render. The literal `agr_curation_query` token is still present in the base prompt.

phrase: Structured output is enforced as `GeneResultEnvelope`. Keep prose concise and compatible with that schema.
synonyms: produce JSON matching GeneResultEnvelope; the structured-output layer below is authoritative for final response shape
reason: GV-35 de-dup. The pre-rewrite '# Output' opener restated the JSON-only / GeneResultEnvelope output mandate that the locked core already injects ('Output contract from agent.yaml: produce JSON matching GeneResultEnvelope; the structured-output layer below is authoritative for final response shape' plus the CRITICAL structured-output block). The rewrite drops the base restatement; the mandate survives in the core half of the render. The gene-object FIELD detail (gene_id/symbol/taxon/species/data_provider + optional fields) is KEPT in <result_contract> because the core does not enumerate it.

synonyms: It offers many specific methods — search for a gene, fetch an allele by ID
reason: GV-24 de-dup (tool MECHANICS vs JUDGMENT). The pre-rewrite '# Available Gene Methods' block restated per-method tool mechanics (required/optional param lists, recommended-use one-liners) that the curator tool catalog already carries: agr_curation_query's bindings.yaml `methods.search_genes.description` ('Find genes whose symbol contains the text you give'), `methods.get_gene_by_exact_symbol.description` ('Find the gene whose official symbol exactly matches'), `methods.search_genes_bulk.description`, and `methods.get_gene_by_id.description`, under a tool whose top-level summary states it 'offers many specific methods — search for a gene, fetch an allele by ID ... Different agents use the methods that fit their job'. The rewrite keeps the curation JUDGMENT (which method to choose when, in <lookup_workflow>) and drops the restated mechanics. The synonym matches the tool's bindings description/summary (the harness home text).

GV-24 de-dup. Same lever as the search_genes method block: the per-method headers and param lists of '# Available Gene Methods' are tool mechanics carried by the agr_curation_query bindings catalog (`methods.get_gene_by_exact_symbol`: 'Find the gene whose official symbol exactly matches'). The which-method-when judgment ('An exact official database symbol you are already confident in -> get_gene_by_exact_symbol') is KEPT in <lookup_workflow>. The synonym is the bindings description's 'many specific lookup methods' phrasing (the home text).

phrase: Searches using LIKE patterns: exact, prefix, then contains.
synonyms: matches by exact, then prefix, then contains (case-insensitive)
reason: GV-24 (Phase C review follow-up). The exact->prefix->contains search-order mechanic is STRATEGY-AFFECTING, not dead implementation detail: the surviving troubleshooting step in <lookup_workflow> ('narrow by adding more characters') depends on the model understanding why a short query like 'daf' returns many candidates. So it is RELOCATED (not deleted) to the model-facing agr_curation_query tool documentation: the @function_tool docstring in packages/alliance/python/src/agr_ai_curation_alliance/tools/agr_curation.py (model-facing) AND the bindings.yaml agr_curation_query metadata.documentation.summary (curator-facing + the harness home text). The synonym is the verbatim phrasing now in the bindings summary, which is what the bindings:agr_curation_query home-check reads (top-level description + documentation.summary).

GV-24 (Phase C review follow-up). Case-insensitivity travels with the same search-order mechanic above and is relocated with it to the model-facing agr_curation_query docstring and the bindings.yaml summary, rather than deleted, because the 'adding characters narrows the candidate set' guidance only makes sense alongside the (case-insensitive) exact->prefix->contains model. The synonym is the verbatim '(case-insensitive)' phrasing now in the bindings summary (the bindings:agr_curation_query home text).

phrase: Returns a `match_type` field showing how it matched.
reason: GV-24 (pure internal mechanic; preserved elsewhere). The fact that the search returns a match_type is a tool-output mechanic; the match_type value itself survives as a documented field of the result-contract `gene_candidates` detail (GV-32, kept in <result_contract>: 'gene_candidates: ... and match_type'). The standalone 'Returns a match_type field showing how it matched' restatement under the method block is dropped with no home (printed for review).
GV-REPO. A developer-facing repository link is not a curation rule, not curator-voice content, and instructs the model on nothing. Dropped with no home (printed for review).
